page.title: Enjoy Authentic Paella Onboard: Marbella Yacht Charter Dining

data.meta_description: Book a Marbella yacht charter with paella onboard. Fresh seafood, private chef, and stunning sea views from Puerto Banús. Prices from €749/2h.
data.summary: Discover the best Marbella yacht charter paella onboard experiences. From intimate speedboat trips to luxury superyachts, enjoy authentic Spanish paella served fresh on the Mediterranean.
key_facts: Paella onboard available on all our yachts departing Puerto Banús
Choose from classic seafood, Valencian, or vegetarian paella options
Private chef service included for groups of 5-12 guests
Prices start from €749 for 2 hours on Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39
All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and VAT
Mangusta 80 charters (4h min) include a free jet ski
Paella is freshly cooked on board or prepared shoreside
Dietary requirements catered for with advance notice

faq.q: Can I have paella cooked onboard during a yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes, we can arrange for a private chef to prepare authentic paella onboard your charter. This is available on all our yachts departing from Puerto Banús, including the Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, and Mangusta 80 variants. The chef uses fresh local ingredients and cooks the paella on a gas burner on the aft deck or in the galley, depending on the vessel.

faq.q: What types of paella are available on a Marbella yacht charter?
faq.a: We offer several paella options: classic seafood paella with prawns, mussels, and clams; Valencian paella with chicken and rabbit; and vegetarian paella with seasonal vegetables. All are made with Bomba rice, saffron, and olive oil. Let us know your preference when booking, and we will confirm availability.

faq.q: How much does a yacht charter with paella cost in Marbella?
faq.a: Prices vary by boat. Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours, including skipper, drinks, snacks, and VAT. Tier B boats (Mangusta 80) start at €4,719 for 4 hours with a free jet ski. Paella catering is an additional charge, typically €25-€45 per person depending on the menu.

faq.q: Can I bring my own paella ingredients onboard?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own ingredients and cook paella yourself, provided the yacht has a suitable galley or outdoor cooking area. However, we recommend using our private chef service for convenience and authenticity. The chef will handle all preparation, cooking, and cleanup.

faq.q: How many guests can enjoy paella on a Marbella yacht charter?
faq.a: The number of guests depends on the yacht. The Dubhe and Mariah SX21 (5 pax) are suitable for small groups. The Azimut 39 (11 pax) and Mangusta 80 (12 pax) are ideal for larger parties. For groups of 10-12, we recommend the Azimut 58 Flybridge, Ferretti 94, or Canados 86.

faq.q: Is paella included in the standard charter price?
faq.a: No, paella is not included in the standard charter price. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and VAT. Paella is an optional extra that can be added at an additional cost.

faq.q: Do you cater for dietary restrictions with paella onboard?
faq.a: Absolutely. We can accommodate gluten-free, dairy-free, and vegan diets with advance notice. For example, we offer a vegan paella with artichokes, peppers, and peas. Please inform us of any allergies or dietary requirements at the time of booking so the chef can prepare accordingly.

faq.q: What other food options are available on a Marbella yacht charter?
faq.a: Besides paella, we can arrange tapas platters, grilled seafood, barbecues, or full-course dinners. Our standard charter includes light snacks such as olives, crisps, and nuts. For a more substantial meal, we recommend the paella option or a bespoke menu from our catering partners.

data.body_html:
Few experiences capture the essence of the Costa del Sol like enjoying a plate of authentic Spanish paella while cruising the Mediterranean. A Marbella yacht charter paella onboard combines the best of both worlds: the luxury of a private boat and the rich culinary tradition of Spain. Departing from Puerto Banús, you can sail past the Golden Mile, anchor in a secluded cove like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés, and watch your chef prepare a steaming pan of saffron-infused rice. Whether you are celebrating a special occasion or simply indulging in a day on the water, this is dining at its finest.

Why Choose a Marbella Yacht Charter with Paella Onboard?

Marbella is synonymous with sunshine, glamour, and world-class cuisine. When you combine a yacht charter with paella, you get an unforgettable day out. The warm sea breeze, the sound of waves lapping against the hull, and the aroma of sizzling seafood create a sensory experience no restaurant can match. Our yachts depart from Puerto Banús, the iconic marina lined with designer boutiques and chic cafes. From there, you can cruise along the coast to Estepona or Sotogrande, or drop anchor near La Concha mountain for stunning views. With prices starting from €749 for two hours on the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, this is an accessible luxury for groups of up to 12 guests.

Our Fleet for Paella Onboard Experiences

We offer a range of yachts suitable for paella dining, from intimate speedboats to spacious superyachts. Each vessel is equipped with a galley or outdoor cooking area, and our chefs bring portable gas burners for authentic paella preparation. Below is a selection of our most popular options for a Marbella yacht charter paella onboard:

YachtLengthMax GuestsPrice (2h)
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749
Azimut 3912.5m11€749
Mangusta 80 (Grey/White)24m12From €4,719 (4h)
Azimut 58 Flybridge17.6m12On request
Ferretti 9429m12On request

For smaller groups, the Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) or Mariah SX21 (6.4m, 5 pax) are perfect for an intimate paella lunch. Larger parties can book the Canados 86 (26m, 12 pax) or Maiora 26m (26m, 12 pax). All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT. Paella catering is an additional cost, typically €25–€45 per person.

What Paella Options Are Available?

Our private chefs specialise in traditional Spanish paella. You can choose from:

  • Seafood paella – prawns, mussels, clams, and squid, cooked in a rich fish stock.
  • Valencian paella – chicken, rabbit, green beans, and garrofó beans, the original recipe.
  • Vegetarian paella – artichokes, peppers, peas, and seasonal vegetables.

All paellas are made with Bomba rice, saffron, pimentón, and extra virgin olive oil. The chef cooks the paella in a traditional wide pan, allowing the rice to absorb the flavours while developing a crispy socarrat at the bottom. Dietary requirements such as gluten-free or vegan can be accommodated with advance notice.

How to Book Your Paella Yacht Charter in Marbella

Booking a Marbella yacht charter paella onboard is straightforward. Visit our homepage to browse the full fleet, or contact us directly via WhatsApp for pricing on specific yachts. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance to ensure chef availability and ingredient sourcing. You can also read our guide to paella onboard for more tips. For larger groups, consider the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) which includes a free jet ski on 4-hour charters – perfect for adding water sports to your culinary day.

Best Spots to Anchor for Paella Dining

Marbella’s coastline offers numerous anchorages ideal for a paella lunch. Popular spots include:

  • Cala del Faro – a sheltered cove near Cabopino, with clear waters and sandy seabed.
  • Cala Cortés – a quiet bay between Marbella and Estepona, surrounded by pine-clad hills.
  • Río Verde – just west of Puerto Banús, offering calm waters and views of the Golden Mile.
  • Estepona Marina – a vibrant stop with easy access to shore for provisions.

Your skipper will choose the best anchorage based on wind and sea conditions, ensuring a smooth and enjoyable dining experience. For more ideas, check our paella charter guide.

What’s Included in Your Charter?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks (olives, crisps, nuts), insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). The paella service is an optional extra. For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39), the 2-hour charter at €749 is ideal for a short sunset cruise with tapas, while the 8-hour charter at €2,299 allows for a full day of swimming, sunbathing, and paella lunch. Tier B boats (Mangusta 80) require a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719 and include a free jet ski. For other yachts, contact us for a custom quote.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I have paella cooked onboard during a yacht charter in Marbella?

Yes, we can arrange for a private chef to prepare authentic paella onboard your charter. This is available on all our yachts departing from Puerto Banús, including the Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, and Mangusta 80 variants. The chef uses fresh local ingredients and cooks the paella on a gas burner on the aft deck or in the galley, depending on the vessel.

What types of paella are available on a Marbella yacht charter?

We offer several paella options: classic seafood paella with prawns, mussels, and clams; Valencian paella with chicken and rabbit; and vegetarian paella with seasonal vegetables. All are made with Bomba rice, saffron, and olive oil. Let us know your preference when booking, and we will confirm availability.

How much does a yacht charter with paella cost in Marbella?

Prices vary by boat. Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours, including skipper, drinks, snacks, and VAT. Tier B boats (Mangusta 80) start at €4,719 for 4 hours with a free jet ski. Paella catering is an additional charge, typically €25-€45 per person depending on the menu.

Can I bring my own paella ingredients onboard?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own ingredients and cook paella yourself, provided the yacht has a suitable galley or outdoor cooking area. However, we recommend using our private chef service for convenience and authenticity. The chef will handle all preparation, cooking, and cleanup.

How many guests can enjoy paella on a Marbella yacht charter?

The number of guests depends on the yacht. The Dubhe and Mariah SX21 (5 pax) are suitable for small groups. The Azimut 39 (11 pax) and Mangusta 80 (12 pax) are ideal for larger parties. For groups of 10-12, we recommend the Azimut 58 Flybridge, Ferretti 94, or Canados 86.

Is paella included in the standard charter price?

No, paella is not included in the standard charter price. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and VAT. Paella is an optional extra that can be added at an additional cost.

Do you cater for dietary restrictions with paella onboard?

Absolutely. We can accommodate gluten-free, dairy-free, and vegan diets with advance notice. For example, we offer a vegan paella with artichokes, peppers, and peas. Please inform us of any allergies or dietary requirements at the time of booking so the chef can prepare accordingly.

What other food options are available on a Marbella yacht charter?

Besides paella, we can arrange tapas platters, grilled seafood, barbecues, or full-course dinners. Our standard charter includes light snacks such as olives, crisps, and nuts. For a more substantial meal, we recommend the paella option or a bespoke menu from our catering partners.
